case_metadata.user_request: Find the lowest-energy adsorption configuration for hydroxyl radical on the provided surface.

analysis_json.message: Most stable config adsorption energy: -2.4185 eV. Target Atom: O (from plan index 0, global index 54 in relaxed structure). -> Nearest: Pd (Index 39), Distance: 2.085 Å (Threshold: 2.972), Bound: True. Bonded Surface Atoms: Pd-2.085Å, Pd-2.093Å, Pd-2.503Å. Is Bound: True. Reactivity Change Detected: False (Bond Changes: 0). plan.reasoning: History shows ontop Pd (index 0) was tested but proved unstable (chemical slip to hollow FCC), yielding E=-2.381 eV as current best; this site type must be forbidden. SMILES [OH] matches the provided RDKit data: O (index 0, SP3, radical_electrons=1) so ReactiveSpecies binding must occur at index [0]. No other sites have been explicitly planned yet. To locate a potentially lower-energy configuration we therefore target the hollow site that the adsorbate spontaneously relaxed into, using surface_binding_atoms ['Pd','Pd','Pd']. This satisfies all site-alignment rules (hollow accepts 1 binding index) and avoids the forbidden ontop-Pd geometry.
